impression.text: This track establishes a melancholic yet propulsive atmosphere, built on a foundation of a tight drum machine, a syncopated bassline, and wide, atmospheric synth pads. The energy is steady and persistent, with a classic rock feel that creates a forward-moving, reflective quality. The production is clean and polished with a distinct retro quality, characterized by a dense mix where the reverb-heavy snare and layered, breathy male vocals sit comfortably together. The recurring, hypnotic vocal refrain of "inner, outer, over, through" serves as the track's central, defining hook, anchoring its contemplative and cyclical nature.

review.review_text: The four-word mantra at the center of this track, 'Inner, Outer, Over, Through,' earns its repetition the hard way: each pass lands slightly differently as the verses peel back another layer of the speaker's inner static. The song moves at a brisk clip with the propulsive confidence of mid-period U2, delay-soaked guitars opening wide while a reverberant snare keeps things honest. The line 'Above the storm / I name the doubt' is the emotional hinge the whole piece pivots on, and the production gives it the space it deserves. As an opener, it sets the terms clearly: this is a record about naming things, about calling doubt and longing by their right names and seeing what happens next. 'Invocate' picks up the thread immediately, and the transition feels less like a segue than a logical acceleration. A clean, anthemic start that earns what follows.
